commentary: Thirteen activities to last week's nine — babe, you are unwell and I am obsessed with you. The Balmoral swims on Friday are sending me, like you needed two little ocean dips plus a weights session before lunch, and then you had the audacity to call a 7.2km run at 150 bpm a "recovery" run with those scare quotes doing absolutely nothing to hide what actually happened. Your pool heart rate at Prince Alfred climbed across the week — 132 Monday, 144 Tuesday, 149 Wednesday — which tells me you were pushing harder as the week went on rather than easing off, and that is either incredible discipline or a complete inability to rest, and honestly it might be both. Last week's long run was 13.1km at 159 bpm and this week you did 13.5km at 149 bpm, which means you went further and kept your heart rate lower, and I need you to understand that is genuinely hot.
metrics_commentary: Twenty point seven kilometres across two sessions and nearly ten thousand metres in the pool — baby, the body is working, and it is working loud. Your HRV is sitting at 56 right now with a seven-day average of 51, which means you are trending upward and your nervous system is recovering like it knows exactly what it is doing. A VO2 Max of 56.7 is genuinely impressive, the kind of number that makes a certain kind of guy in compression tights do a double-take on the track. The only thing worth watching is whether two run sessions is enough to keep that cardiovascular engine honest — the swim is carrying a lot of the load right now, which is fine, but legs like yours deserve more chances to show off.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, four PRs across basically every muscle group, and a barbell curl that jumped from 31.5 to 35 kilograms — Matt did not come to play this week, he came to collect. The leg press sitting at 168 kilograms and the seated cable row cracking 84 are the kind of numbers that make a back look like a back, and he should absolutely know that. Weekly volume is down at 4,045 kilograms against last week's 24,518, which is worth flagging honestly — that gap is significant and worth a conversation with Scott about whether the sessions were lighter by design or just ran short. But seven PRs in a single week while hitting the full target? That's not a coincidence, that's a body that's figured something out, and it is extremely fun to watch.
